name: ETH 1h Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: price dislocates away from fast value, reclaims it, and the position scales in only when the reclaim improves on the next bar
thesis: ETH 1h is the primary lane and sits in a chop-to-transition mix. That should produce repeated failed extensions and value recoveries, but the all-in first reclaim is fragile. A scale-in only after the reclaim proves itself gives denser participation without the ceremonial sparsity that killed earlier cycles.
invalidation_condition: If reclaim events remain too sparse, or if the second-step confirmation almost never arrives often enough to improve expectancy, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price stretches away from value, reclaims it as inventory resets, stabilizes for one more bar, and then rotates toward deeper balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In transitionary 1h structure, offside inventory should frequently unwind through value. The add-on only after improved confirmation should remove some bad first-reclaim noise without eliminating too many trades.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is the best fit because it is the cycle primary lane, active enough to create repeated reset events, and clean enough that a reclaim-plus-improvement sequence can be observed.

indicator_role_map.EMA_55: deeper balance target and remaining-position anchor

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim may occur inside a genuine breakout, causing the add-on to increase size just as mean-reversion logic fails.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the base setup works but the add-on hurts expectancy, keep the reclaim trigger and tighten or remove only the scale-in condition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Many first-target captures, a smaller set of second-target completions, and limited dependence on long runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 1h shows non-sparse reclaim participation, first partials clear costs regularly, and the add-on improves average trade quality.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or add-on confirmation that rarely appears or consistently worsens outcomes.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low because the core trigger is stretch plus reclaim, not a ritual chain

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for scale-in around value recovery.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 1h shows enough reclaim-and-improve sequences with positive post-cost first-target behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h remains too noisy or too sparse for the add-on logic to matter.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: High enough count to validate the reclaim thesis, with most edge coming from first and second partials.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks portability on a similar liquid major with different volatility amplitude.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h preserves reclaim-and-improve structure with cleaner execution.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h suppresses the mechanism into low-density irrelevance.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count but cleaner recoveries than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same logic survives structurally on a slower lane without collapsing back into sparse behavior.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still trades enough to preserve the reclaim-and-improve thesis.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the mechanism only works at denser 1h resolution.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, same role separation, smaller impact from the scale-in tranche.

long: Add 40% when next bar closes >= EMA_20 and remains above VWAP
short: Add 40% when next bar closes <= EMA_20 and remains below VWAP

long: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 below initial entry
Exit remaining 25% on touch of EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 above initial entry
Exit remaining 25% on touch of EMA_55 or 5 bars
